findings: The adult male is identifiable as a Common Grackle: lanky blackbird proportions, long legs and tail, pale-yellow eye, glossy blue-purple head, bronzy-iridescent body, and long heavy bill agree with the references and live identification pages.
Taxonomy is correct: Common Grackle, Quiscalus quiscula, family Icteridae.
All printed measurements are correct: length 28–34 cm (11.0–13.4 in), wingspan 36–46 cm (14.2–18.1 in), and weight 74–142 g (2.6–5.0 oz).
The habitat, behavior, and six written field marks are consistent with the verified sources.
Minor anatomical issue: the bill is somewhat too deep, blunt, and pale gray compared with the slimmer, darker bill in the field photographs.
Minor anatomical issue: the feet and strongly hooked claws are oversized and slightly raptorial relative to the reference birds.
Minor field-mark issue: the illustrated tail is broadly fanned rather than clearly showing the species' typical narrow, keeled or shallow-V profile.
The main bird has one head, one beak, two wings with the far wing largely obscured by the lateral pose, two legs, and one tail; no duplicated or missing anatomy is evident.
The auxiliary wing study is cropped tightly at the left edge, slightly weakening an otherwise clear, balanced, and legible composition.
No visible factual text is materially erroneous, and no place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Common Grackle Identification — All About Birds
